rationale: Haaijer et al. (2000) is cited multiple times in the paper's core argument, especially to motivate why individual decision time should matter for choice-model predictability and error. The citation is also used in methodological setup, where the authors note and control for order-related declines in decision time. This is more than a passing mention because decision-time effects are a central pillar of the study's framing and estimation approach.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Haaijer et al. (2000) as an important prior foundation for incorporating response latency/decision time into conjoint choice modeling. It builds on that precedent to motivate its decision-time hypotheses and related timing controls within the CAM logit framework.
